name: Burnt Sugar Cake
recipeInstructions:
For the syrup, in a small heavy skillet or saucepan, heat sugar over medium heat. Lower to medium-low as syrup forms; stir frequently to break up lumps. Cook and stir until brown syrup forms and mixture begins to smoke, about 30 minutes. Gradually stir in boiling water. Remove from heat; cool. Reserve half for cake frosting. Preheat oven to 375°F.
For the cake, cream butter and sugar together in a mixing bowl until light, about 3 minutes. Gradually beat in half cup burnt syrup. Add vanilla, then egg yolks one at a time, beating after each addition. Sift flour, baking powder, and salt together. Add flour mixture alternately with milk, beating batter until smooth. Beat egg whites until stiff, then gently fold by hand into batter. Pour batter into two 8-inch oiled layer cake pans, smoothing batter. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes. Remove from oven and cool. Makes 12 servings. Frost with Butter Cream Frosting or Seven-minute burnt sugar frosting.
